prompts.abstract: A cylindrical object with a flat circular top and bottom. The top surface is slightly recessed compared to the bottom.
prompts.description: A cylindrical object with a flat circular top and bottom. The top surface is slightly recessed compared to the bottom.
prompts.beginner: The design involves creating a small, flat cylinder with a square base. The cylinder stands 0.0521 units tall and has a base that measures 0.75 units on each side. This simple shape serves as a foundational component in the overall design.
prompts.intermediate: Start by creating a new coordinate system aligned with the default axes. On the X-Y plane, draw a circular face with a center point and a specified radius. Scale down the entire sketch uniformly to achieve the desired size. Transform the scaled sketch into a three-dimensional object by extruding it along the Z-axis to create a solid body. The resulting cylindrical base has a height of 0.0521 units and a square cross-section with sides measuring 0.75 units. This process forms the foundational element of the design, which can be further modified or combined with other components.
prompts.expert: Create a new coordinate system with Euler angles set to zero degrees and a translation vector also set to zero. On the X-Y plane, initiate a two-dimensional sketch and draw a single face with one loop. Within this loop, draw a circle centered at coordinates (0.375, 0.375) with a radius of 0.375 units. Apply a uniform scale factor of 0.75 to the entire sketch to reduce its size proportionally. Ensure the scaled sketch remains aligned with the original coordinate system by rotating it with Euler angles set to zero degrees and translating it with a vector of zero units. To transform the two-dimensional sketch into a three-dimensional model, extrude the sketch along the normal direction to a depth of 0.0521 units. Ensure this extrusion forms a new solid body. The final dimensions of the cylindrical base will be 0.75 units in length, 0.75 units in width, and 0.0521 units in height.
prompts.all_level_data:
<level1>

On the X-Y plane, initiate a two-dimensional sketch and draw a single face with one loop. Within this loop, draw a circle centered at coordinates (0.375, 0.375) with a radius of 0.375 units. Apply a uniform scale factor of 0.75 to the entire sketch to reduce its size proportionally. Ensure the scaled sketch remains aligned with the original coordinate system by rotating it with Euler angles set to zero degrees and translating it with a vector of zero units. To transform the two-dimensional sketch into a three-dimensional model, extrude the sketch along the normal direction to a depth of 0.0521 units. Ensure this extrusion forms a new solid body. The final dimensions of the cylindrical base will be 0.75 units in length, 0.75 units in width, and 0.0521 units in height.
</level1>

Scale down the entire sketch uniformly to achieve the desired size. Transform the scaled sketch into a three-dimensional object by extruding it along the Z-axis to create a solid body. The resulting cylindrical base has a height of 0.0521 units and a square cross-section with sides measuring 0.75 units. This process forms the foundational element of the design, which can be further modified or combined with other components.

prompts.nli_data:
<part_1>
### Construct a Cylindrical Base
#### Create a New Coordinate System
- Set Euler Angles to [0.0, 0.0, 0.0].
- Set Translation Vector to [0.0, 0.0, 0.0].

#### Draw a 2D Sketch
- Create a new sketch on the XY plane.
- Draw a single face with one loop.
- In the loop, draw a circle:
  - Center at (0.375, 0.375).
  - Radius of 0.375.

#### Scale the 2D Sketch
- Apply a scale factor of 0.75 to the entire sketch.

#### Transform the Scaled 2D Sketch into 3D
- Rotate the sketch using the Euler Angles [0.0, 0.0, 0.0].
- Translate the sketch using the Translation Vector [0.0, 0.0, 0.0].

#### Extrude the 2D Sketch to Generate the 3D Model
- Extrude the sketch to a depth of 0.0521 units in the direction of the normal.
- Do not extrude in the opposite direction of the normal.
- Ensure this extrusion creates a new solid body.

#### Final Dimensions
- Length: 0.75 units
- Width: 0.75 units
- Height: 0.0521 units

</part_1>
